title: 깜빡 속을 뻔했어

goal.ko: 상황을 과장하거나 진심을 살짝 숨기고, 결국 인정하는 표현을 자연스럽게 할 수 있어요
goal.en: Express 'almost,' fake a reaction, and then half-admit the truth

key_points: -(으)ㄹ 뻔하다 — I almost did X (but didn't)

script.speaker: 카요
script.en: Bro! What happens when a king falls over?

script.speaker: 카요
script.text: 형 벌써 웃었어! 안 웃긴 척하지 마.
script.en: You already laughed! Stop pretending it's not funny.

script.speaker: 리암
script.en: Okay, it was somewhat funny. I admit it!

script.speaker: 리암
script.en: Now it's my turn. What happens when a banana laughs?

script.speaker: 카요
script.text: 형 너무 아재야! 어려 보여서 깜빡 속을 뻔했어.
script.en: You're such an 아재! You look young so I almost got fooled.

vocab.english: to be funny, to crack someone up
to be fooled, to be tricked
middle-aged man vibe; someone with cheesy/dad humor
momentarily, without realizing, in a flash
I admit it! / Fair enough! / You got me!

grammar.title: -(으)ㄹ 뻔하다
grammar.explanation_en: Something came very close to happening — but ultimately didn't. The near-miss is the point. Usually used in past tense (뻔했어). Works for both good and bad near-misses!
grammar.pattern: Vowel/ㄹ stem + -ㄹ 뻔하다 / Consonant stem + -을 뻔하다

scene_example.en: Oh man. I almost laughed (but didn't).

rule_boxes.label: 🔵 Vowel / ㄹ stem → -ㄹ 뻔하다
rule_boxes.content:
웃을 뻔했어 = I almost laughed
울 뻔했어 = I almost cried
넘어질 뻔했어 = I almost fell

rule_boxes.label: 🟢 Consonant stem → -을 뻔하다
rule_boxes.content:
속을 뻔했어 = I almost got fooled
먹을 뻔했어 = I almost ate it
잊을 뻔했어 = I almost forgot

rule_boxes.label: 🟡 깜빡 + 뻔하다 — the 'without noticing' combo
rule_boxes.content:
깜빡 속을 뻔했어 = I almost got fooled without realizing
깜빡 웃을 뻔했어 = I almost laughed before I knew it
깜빡 = adds the 'slipped past my guard' nuance

rule_boxes.label: 🔴 KEY: it did NOT happen!
rule_boxes.content:
웃을 뻔했어 = I almost laughed (→ but I didn't)
웃었어 = I laughed (→ it happened)
뻔하다 always means the action was avoided or narrowly missed

examples.en: You look young so I almost got fooled without realizing.
I almost missed the bus. Thank goodness!

grammar.title: -는 척하다
grammar.explanation_en: Act as if you're doing or feeling X when you're really not — or pretend something isn't the case. Great for calling out fake reactions (like pretending a joke wasn't funny)!
grammar.pattern: Action verb + -는 척하다 / Desc verb + -(으)ㄴ 척하다 / Past + -(으)ㄴ 척하다

scene_example.ko: 형 벌써 웃었어! 안 웃긴 척하지 마.
scene_example.en: You already laughed! Stop pretending it's not funny.

rule_boxes.label: 🔵 Action verb + -는 척하다
rule_boxes.content:
안 웃긴 척하지 마. = Don't pretend it's not funny.
모르는 척했어. = I pretended not to know.
자는 척해. = He's pretending to be asleep.

rule_boxes.label: 🟢 Descriptive verb + -(으)ㄴ 척하다
rule_boxes.content:
바쁜 척해요. = I'm acting busy.
아픈 척했어. = She faked being sick.
괜찮은 척했는데 힘들었어. = I acted like I was fine but it was hard.

rule_boxes.label: 🟡 Past tense + -(으)ㄴ 척하다
rule_boxes.content:
먹은 척했어. = I pretended I ate.
공부한 척했어. = I pretended I studied.
들은 척도 안 해. = She's not even pretending to listen.

examples.ko: 리암이 모르는 척했는데 다 알고 있었어.
examples.en: Liam pretended not to know, but he knew everything.
She acts busy. She actually has plenty of time.

grammar.title: -기는 하다
grammar.explanation_en: One of the subtlest patterns in Korean — you admit something is true, but with a built-in reservation or 'but.' Three main nuances: (1) reluctant admission, (2) contrast with 'but,' (3) mild confirmation.

scene_example.en: Okay, it WAS somewhat funny. I admit it!

rule_boxes.label: 🔵 Nuance 1 — Reluctant admission
rule_boxes.content:
좀 웃기기는 했다. = It was somewhat funny (okay, I'll admit it)
맛있기는 해. = It IS tasty (not thrilled to say it)
인정하기 싫지만 — but you have to

rule_boxes.label: 🟢 Nuance 2 — Contrast ('it IS... but')
rule_boxes.content:
웃기기는 한데 너무 아재야. = It's funny, BUT so cheesy.
예쁘기는 한데 비싸. = It's pretty, but expensive.
하기는 했는데 잘 모르겠어. = I did do it, but I'm not sure.

rule_boxes.label: 🟡 Nuance 3 — Mild confirmation
rule_boxes.content:
그렇기는 하지. = That IS true (I suppose).
맞기는 해. = You're right (I have to admit).
좋기는 좋아. = It is good (emphasizing the fact).

rule_boxes.label: 🔴 -기는 하다 vs -기는커녕
rule_boxes.content:
기는 하다 = admits it's (somewhat) true
기는커녕 = 'Far from it!' (completely denies)
웃기기는 했어. = It was somewhat funny.
웃기기는커녕 화가 났어. = Far from funny, I was angry.

examples.en: Okay, it was somewhat funny. I admit it!
It IS delicious, but it's too spicy.
I did study, but the test was too hard.

banmal_jondaemal.intro_ko: 이 에피소드에서 카요(동생)는 리암(형)에게 반말을 써요. 같은 표현을 어른이나 처음 만난 사람에게 할 때는 어떻게 달라질까요?
banmal_jondaemal.intro_en: In this episode, Kayo uses casual speech with his older brother Liam. How do these expressions change when speaking to an adult or someone you've just met?

rows.english: I almost got fooled without realizing.

vocab_comparison.title_en: How to react to a joke in Korean
vocab_comparison.intro_ko: 농담이나 아재개그에 반응할 때 쓸 수 있는 표현들이에요.
vocab_comparison.intro_en: Expressions for reacting to jokes and dad puns.

table.en: I admit it! / You got me!

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: 아까 부장님 농담에 웃을 뻔했는데 참았어요.
lines.en: I almost laughed at the manager's joke earlier but held it in.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: 저도요. 안 웃긴 척하기가 쉽지 않더라고요.
lines.en: Me too. It's not easy to pretend it's not funny.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: 맞아요. 부장님 완전 아재개그인데 깜빡 속을 뻔했어요.
lines.en: Right. It's total dad humor but I almost got fooled.

items.note: 뻔했네 → [뻔핸네] (ㅎ + ㄴ → 연음화, 받침 ㅎ이 다음 음절과 연결)
안 웃긴 → 부정 안+동사 형용사형. '웃기다'의 관형사형은 '웃긴' (ㄴ 불규칙 없음)
아저씨의 비공식 축약형. 아재개그 = 아저씨 스타일의 개그. 젊은 세대가 주로 씀

messages.text: 아니 진짜 안 웃겼어 인정 안 해

real_life.title: 📖 읽기 — 한국의 아재개그 문화
real_life.passage: 한국에서 '아재개그'는 아저씨들이 좋아하는 말장난을 뜻해요. '아재'는 아저씨를 친근하게 부르는 말이에요. 아재개그는 보통 너무 쉽고 예상 가능해서 웃기지 않은 척하고 싶지만, 깜빡 웃을 뻔한 경우가 많아요. 요즘은 아재개그가 유행해서 젊은 사람들도 즐겨요. 웃기기는 하지만 '아재야!'라고 놀리는 것도 문화의 일부가 됐어요.

quiz.audio_text: 형 너무 아재야! 어려 보여서 깜빡 속을 뻔했어.
quiz.question: 카요가 거의 한 것은?

self_check: '웃을 뻔했어'처럼 거의 일어난 일을 '-(으)ㄹ 뻔하다'로 표현할 수 있어요.
'안 웃긴 척하지 마'처럼 가식이나 연기를 지적할 때 '-는 척하다'를 쓸 수 있어요.
'웃기기는 했다'처럼 마지못해 인정할 때 '-기는 하다'의 뉘앙스를 알아요.
-기는 하다의 3가지 뉘앙스 (인정 / 대조 / 확인)를 구별할 수 있어요.
웃다, 웃기다, 속다, 아재, 깜빡, 인정! 같은 유머 표현을 알아요.

usage.heading: '-(으)ㄹ 뻔하다' — 일어나지 않았다는 게 핵심!
usage.content: The whole point of 뻔하다 is that the action was narrowly avoided. Never use it for something that actually happened.

examples.en: I almost laughed. (= I didn't laugh)
I almost fell. (= I didn't fall)

usage.heading: '-기는 하다' 뉘앙스 3종 세트
usage.content: The same pattern has three flavors depending on context — all involve some kind of concession or admission.

examples.en: It was somewhat funny. (I admit it)
It IS funny, but it's so cheesy.

usage.heading: 인정! — 한국 구어체 표현
usage.content: '인정' literally means 'recognition/acknowledgment.' Used in casual speech to mean 'I admit it!' or 'Fair enough!' Very popular among younger Koreans.

examples.en: Fine, I admit it! It was a bit funny.
I can't admit to that one.
